title: Latent Reasoning VLA: Latent Thinking and Prediction for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models benefit from chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, but existing approaches incur high inference overhead and rely on discrete reasoning representations that mismatch continuous perception and control. We propose Latent Reasoning VLA (\textbf{LaRA-VLA}), a unified VLA framework that internalizes multi-modal CoT reasoning into continuous latent representations for embodied action. LaRA-VLA performs unified reasoning and prediction in latent space, eliminating explicit CoT generation at inference time and enabling efficient, action-oriented control. To realize latent embodied reasoning, we introduce a curriculum-based training paradigm that progressively transitions from explicit textual and visual CoT supervision to latent reasoning, and finally adapts latent reasoning dynamics to condition action generation. We construct two structured CoT datasets and evaluate LaRA-VLA on both simulation benchmarks and long-horizon real-robot manipulation tasks. Experimental results show that LaRA-VLA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art VLA methods while reducing inference latency by up to 90\% compared to explicit CoT-based approaches, demonstrating latent reasoning as an effective and efficient paradigm for real-time embodied control. Project Page: \href{https://loveju1y.github.io/Latent-Reasoning-VLA/}{LaRA-VLA Website}.
tldr: The paper introduces LaRA-VLA, a framework that uses continuous latent representations for efficient vision-language-action reasoning, eliminating explicit chain-of-thought generation and achieving significant latency reduction in real-time embodied control.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了LaRA-VLA，一个使用连续隐空间表征进行高效视觉-语言-动作推理的框架，消除了显式的思维链生成，并在实时具身控制中实现了显著的延迟减少。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型受益于思维链 (CoT) 推理，但现有方法会产生高昂的推理开销，并且依赖于离散的推理表示，这与连续的感知和控制不匹配。我们提出了潜在推理 VLA (\textbf{LaRA-VLA})，这是一个统一的 VLA 框架，它将多模态 CoT 推理内化为具身动作的连续潜在表示。LaRA-VLA 在潜在空间中执行统一的推理和预测，消除了推理时显式的 CoT 生成，并实现了高效的、面向动作的控制。为了实现潜在的具身推理，我们引入了一种基于课程学习的训练范式，该范式逐渐从显式的文本和视觉 CoT 监督过渡到潜在推理，并最终调整潜在推理动态以调节动作生成。我们构建了两个结构化的 CoT 数据集，并在模拟基准和长时程真实机器人操作任务上评估 LaRA-VLA。实验结果表明，LaRA-VLA 始终优于最先进的 VLA 方法，同时与基于显式 CoT 的方法相比，推理延迟最多可降低 90%，这证明了潜在推理是实时具身控制的一种有效且高效的范式。项目主页： \href{https://loveju1y.github.io/Latent-Reasoning-VLA/}{LaRA-VLA Website}.

title: StreamVLA: Breaking the Reason-Act Cycle via Completion-State Gating
summary: Long-horizon robotic manipulation requires bridging the gap between high-level planning (System 2) and low-level control (System 1). Current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often entangle these processes, performing redundant multimodal reasoning at every timestep, which leads to high latency and goal instability. To address this, we present StreamVLA, a dual-system architecture that unifies textual task decomposition, visual goal imagination, and continuous action generation within a single parameter-efficient backbone. We introduce a "Lock-and-Gated" mechanism to intelligently modulate computation: only when a sub-task transition is detected, the model triggers slow thinking to generate a textual instruction and imagines the specific visual completion state, rather than generic future frames. Crucially, this completion state serves as a time-invariant goal anchor, making the policy robust to execution speed variations. During steady execution, these high-level intents are locked to condition a Flow Matching action head, allowing the model to bypass expensive autoregressive decoding for 72% of timesteps. This hierarchical abstraction ensures sub-goal focus while significantly reducing inference latency. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that StreamVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance, with a 98.5% success rate on the LIBERO benchmark and robust recovery in real-world interference scenarios, achieving a 48% reduction in latency compared to full-reasoning baselines.
tldr: StreamVLA introduces a dual-system Vision-Language-Action architecture that breaks the reason-act cycle by using "Lock-and-Gated" mechanisms to reduce redundant computation and latency in robotic manipulation, achieving state-of-the-art performance on the LIBERO benchmark.
tldr_zh: StreamVLA 提出了一种双系统视觉-语言-动作架构，通过使用“锁定和门控”机制来打破推理-行动循环，从而减少机器人操作中冗余计算和延迟，并在 LIBERO 基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 长时程机器人操作需要弥合高层规划（系统 2）和底层控制（系统 1）之间的差距。目前的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型通常将这些过程纠缠在一起，在每个时间步执行冗余的多模态推理，导致高延迟和目标不稳定。为了解决这个问题，我们提出 StreamVLA，一个双系统架构，它在一个参数高效的骨干网络中统一了文本任务分解、视觉目标想象和连续动作生成。我们引入了一种“锁定与门控”机制来智能地调节计算：只有当检测到子任务转换时，模型才会触发慢思考以生成文本指令并想象特定的视觉完成状态，而不是通用的未来帧。至关重要的是，这种完成状态充当了一个时不变的目标锚点，使策略对执行速度的变化具有鲁棒性。在稳定的执行过程中，这些高层意图被锁定以调节一个流匹配动作头，允许模型在 72% 的时间步绕过昂贵的自回归解码。这种分层抽象确保了子目标聚焦，同时显著降低了推理延迟。广泛的评估表明，StreamVLA 实现了最先进的性能，在 LIBERO 基准测试中成功率达到 98.5%，并在真实世界的干扰场景中实现了强大的恢复能力，与完全推理的基线相比，延迟降低了 48%。

title: A Systematic Study of Data Modalities and Strategies for Co-training Large Behavior Models for Robot Manipulation
summary: Large behavior models have shown strong dexterous manipulation capabilities by extending imitation learning to large-scale training on multi-task robot data, yet their generalization remains limited by the insufficient robot data coverage. To expand this coverage without costly additional data collection, recent work relies on co-training: jointly learning from target robot data and heterogeneous data modalities. However, how different co-training data modalities and strategies affect policy performance remains poorly understood. We present a large-scale empirical study examining five co-training data modalities: standard vision-language data, dense language annotations for robot trajectories, cross-embodiment robot data, human videos, and discrete robot action tokens across single- and multi-phase training strategies. Our study leverages 4,000 hours of robot and human manipulation data and 50M vision-language samples to train vision-language-action policies. We evaluate 89 policies over 58,000 simulation rollouts and 2,835 real-world rollouts. Our results show that co-training with forms of vision-language and cross-embodiment robot data substantially improves generalization to distribution shifts, unseen tasks, and language following, while discrete action token variants yield no significant benefits. Combining effective modalities produces cumulative gains and enables rapid adaptation to unseen long-horizon dexterous tasks via fine-tuning. Training exclusively on robot data degrades the visiolinguistic understanding of the vision-language model backbone, while co-training with effective modalities restores these capabilities. Explicitly conditioning action generation on chain-of-thought traces learned from co-training data does not improve performance in our simulation benchmark. Together, these results provide practical guidance for building scalable generalist robot policies.
tldr: This paper presents a large-scale empirical study exploring the effects of different data modalities and strategies for co-training large behavior models for robot manipulation. The results provide guidance for building generalist robot policies by showing that vision-language and cross-embodiment robot data improve generalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文通过大规模实验研究了不同数据模式和策略对机器人操作行为模型联合训练的影响。结果表明，视觉-语言和跨具身机器人数据可以提高泛化能力，为构建通用机器人策略提供了指导。
summary_zh: 大型行为模型通过将模仿学习扩展到多任务机器人数据的大规模训练，展现出强大的灵巧操作能力，但其泛化能力仍然受到机器人数据覆盖不足的限制。为了在不增加昂贵数据采集成本的情况下扩大这种覆盖范围，最近的研究依赖于协同训练：联合学习目标机器人数据和异构数据模态。然而，不同的协同训练数据模态和策略如何影响策略性能仍然知之甚少。我们进行了一项大规模的实证研究，考察了五种协同训练数据模态：标准视觉-语言数据、机器人轨迹的密集语言标注、跨具身机器人数据、人类视频以及横跨单阶段和多阶段训练策略的离散机器人动作令牌。我们的研究利用了4000小时的机器人和人类操作数据以及5000万的视觉-语言样本来训练视觉-语言-动作策略。我们在58000次模拟实验和2835次真实世界实验中评估了89个策略。我们的结果表明，与视觉-语言和跨具身机器人数据的协同训练显著提高了对分布偏移、未见任务和语言跟随的泛化能力，而离散动作令牌变体没有产生显著的好处。结合有效的模态会产生累积收益，并通过微调，能够快速适应未见的长程灵巧任务。仅在机器人数据上进行训练会降低视觉-语言模型骨干的视听语言理解能力，而与有效模态的协同训练可以恢复这些能力。明确地以从协同训练数据中学习的思维链追踪为条件来生成动作，并不能提高我们在模拟基准测试中的性能。总之，这些结果为构建可扩展的通用机器人策略提供了实用的指导。

title: Learning Abstractions for Hierarchical Planning in Program-Synthesis Agents
summary: Humans learn abstractions and use them to plan efficiently to quickly generalize across tasks -- an ability that remains challenging for state-of-the-art large language model (LLM) agents and deep reinforcement learning (RL) systems. Inspired by the cognitive science of how people form abstractions and intuitive theories of their world knowledge, Theory-Based RL (TBRL) systems, such as TheoryCoder, exhibit strong generalization through effective use of abstractions. However, they heavily rely on human-provided abstractions and sidestep the abstraction-learning problem. We introduce TheoryCoder-2, a new TBRL agent that leverages LLMs' in-context learning ability to actively learn reusable abstractions rather than relying on hand-specified ones, by synthesizing abstractions from experience and integrating them into a hierarchical planning process. We conduct experiments on diverse environments, including BabyAI, Minihack and VGDL games like Sokoban. We find that TheoryCoder-2 is significantly more sample-efficient than baseline LLM agents augmented with classical planning domain construction, reasoning-based planning, and prior program-synthesis agents such as WorldCoder. TheoryCoder-2 is able to solve complex tasks that the baselines fail, while only requiring minimal human prompts, unlike prior TBRL systems.
tldr: This paper introduces TheoryCoder-2, an LLM-based agent that learns reusable abstractions for hierarchical planning in diverse environments, achieving better sample efficiency and solving more complex tasks compared to SOTA baselines.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为TheoryCoder-2的基于LLM的智能体，它可以在不同的环境中学习可重用的抽象概念，用于分层规划，与最先进的baseline相比，实现了更高的样本效率并解决了更复杂的任务。
summary_zh: 人类学习抽象概念并利用它们进行高效规划，从而快速泛化到不同任务中——这种能力对于最先进的大型语言模型(LLM)智能体和深度强化学习(RL)系统来说仍然具有挑战性。受认知科学关于人们如何形成抽象概念以及对世界知识的直觉理论的启发，基于理论的强化学习(TBRL)系统，如TheoryCoder，通过有效利用抽象概念表现出强大的泛化能力。然而，它们严重依赖于人为提供的抽象概念，并回避了抽象概念学习的问题。我们引入 TheoryCoder-2，一种新型TBRL智能体，它利用 LLM 的上下文学习能力来主动学习可复用的抽象概念，而不是依赖于人工指定的抽象概念，通过从经验中合成抽象概念，并将它们整合到分层规划过程中。我们在多种环境中进行了实验，包括 BabyAI、Minihack 和 VGDL 游戏（如推箱子）。我们发现，与增强了经典规划领域构建的基线 LLM 智能体、基于推理的规划以及先前的程序合成智能体（如 WorldCoder）相比，TheoryCoder-2 的样本效率显著提高。与之前的 TBRL 系统不同，TheoryCoder-2 能够解决基线方法无法解决的复杂任务，同时仅需要最少的人工提示。

title: Mixture-of-World Models: Scaling Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning with Modular Latent Dynamics
summary: A fundamental challenge in multi-task reinforcement learning (MTRL) is achieving sample efficiency in visual domains where tasks exhibit substantial heterogeneity in both observations and dynamics. Model-based reinforcement learning offers a promising path to improved sample efficiency through world models, but standard monolithic architectures struggle to capture diverse task dynamics, resulting in poor reconstruction and prediction accuracy. We introduce Mixture-of-World Models (MoW), a scalable architecture that combines modular variational autoencoders for task-adaptive visual compression, a hybrid Transformer-based dynamics model with task-conditioned experts and a shared backbone, and a gradient-based task clustering strategy for efficient parameter allocation. On the Atari 100k benchmark, a single MoW agent trained once on 26 Atari games achieves a mean human-normalized score of 110.4%, competitive with the score of 114.2% achieved by STORM, an ensemble of 26 task-specific models, while using 50% fewer parameters. On Meta-World, MoW achieves a 74.5% average success rate within 300 thousand environment steps, establishing a new state of the art. These results demonstrate that MoW provides a scalable and parameter-efficient foundation for generalist world models.
tldr: The paper introduces Mixture-of-World Models (MoW), a modular architecture for multi-task reinforcement learning that achieves state-of-the-art performance on Atari 100k and Meta-World benchmarks with improved sample efficiency and parameter efficiency compared to existing methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了混合世界模型（MoW），这是一种用于多任务强化学习的模块化架构，在 Atari 100k 和 Meta-World 基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，并且相比现有方法具有更高的样本效率和参数效率。
summary_zh: 多任务强化学习(MTRL)中的一个根本挑战是在视觉领域实现样本效率，该领域中的任务在观察和动态特性上都表现出显著的异质性。基于模型的强化学习通过世界模型提供了一条提高样本效率的有希望的途径，但标准的单体架构难以捕捉多样化的任务动态，导致较差的重建和预测精度。我们提出了世界模型混合(MoW)，一种可扩展的架构，它结合了用于任务自适应视觉压缩的模块化变分自编码器、一个具有任务条件专家和共享主干的混合Transformer动力学模型，以及一种基于梯度的任务聚类策略，用于高效的参数分配。在Atari 100k基准测试中，一个在26个Atari游戏上训练一次的单个MoW智能体实现了110.4%的平均人类归一化得分，与STORM（由26个任务特定模型组成的集成）所实现的114.2%的得分具有竞争力，同时使用的参数减少了50%。在Meta-World上，MoW在30万环境步内实现了74.5%的平均成功率，确立了新的技术水平。这些结果表明，MoW为通用世界模型提供了一个可扩展且参数高效的基础。

title: OASIS-DC: Generalizable Depth Completion via Output-level Alignment of Sparse-Integrated Monocular Pseudo Depth
summary: Recent monocular foundation models excel at zero-shot depth estimation, yet their outputs are inherently relative rather than metric, limiting direct use in robotics and autonomous driving. We leverage the fact that relative depth preserves global layout and boundaries: by calibrating it with sparse range measurements, we transform it into a pseudo metric depth prior. Building on this prior, we design a refinement network that follows the prior where reliable and deviates where necessary, enabling accurate metric predictions from very few labeled samples. The resulting system is particularly effective when curated validation data are unavailable, sustaining stable scale and sharp edges across few-shot regimes. These findings suggest that coupling foundation priors with sparse anchors is a practical route to robust, deployment-ready depth completion under real-world label scarcity.
tldr: This paper presents a method for depth completion by combining monocular foundation model's relative depth with sparse range measurements, creating a pseudo-metric depth prior and refining it with a network for accurate depth prediction with limited labeled data.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种深度补全方法，通过将单目基础模型的相对深度与稀疏范围测量相结合，创建伪度量深度先验，并使用网络对其进行细化，从而在有限的标记数据下实现精确的深度预测。
summary_zh: 近期单目基础模型在零样本深度估计方面表现出色，但它们的输出本质上是相对深度而非度量深度，限制了其在机器人和自动驾驶中的直接应用。我们利用了相对深度能够保留全局布局和边界的特性：通过使用稀疏距离测量来校准相对深度，我们将其转换为伪度量深度先验。基于此先验，我们设计了一个精细化网络，该网络在可靠区域遵循先验，并在必要时偏离，从而能够从极少的标注样本中实现精确的度量预测。由此产生的系统在缺乏精心策划的验证数据的情况下尤其有效，能够在少量样本情况下保持稳定的尺度和清晰的边缘。这些发现表明，将基础先验与稀疏锚点相结合是解决现实世界标签稀缺情况下鲁棒、可部署的深度补全的一种可行途径。

title: Reinforcement Learning for Active Perception in Autonomous Navigation
summary: This paper addresses the challenge of active perception within autonomous navigation in complex, unknown environments. Revisiting the foundational principles of active perception, we introduce an end-to-end reinforcement learning framework in which a robot must not only reach a goal while avoiding obstacles, but also actively control its onboard camera to enhance situational awareness. The policy receives observations comprising the robot state, the current depth frame, and a particularly local geometry representation built from a short history of depth readings. To couple collision-free motion planning with information-driven active camera control, we augment the navigation reward with a voxel-based information metric. This enables an aerial robot to learn a robust policy that balances goal-directed motion with exploratory sensing. Extensive evaluation demonstrates that our strategy achieves safer flight compared to using fixed, non-actuated camera baselines while also inducing intrinsic exploratory behaviors.
tldr: This paper presents a reinforcement learning framework for autonomous navigation where a robot learns to actively control its camera for enhanced situational awareness, resulting in safer and more exploratory flight.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于自主导航的强化学习框架，其中机器人学习主动控制其摄像头以增强情境感知，从而实现更安全和更具探索性的飞行。
summary_zh: 本文旨在解决复杂未知环境中自主导航中的主动感知问题。通过重新审视主动感知的基本原则，我们引入了一个端到端的强化学习框架，在该框架中，机器人不仅需要在避开障碍物的同时到达目标，还必须主动控制其车载摄像头以增强情境感知能力。该策略接收包含机器人状态、当前深度帧以及由一段短时深度数据构建的局部几何表示的观测值。为了将无碰撞运动规划与信息驱动的主动相机控制相结合，我们使用基于体素的信息度量来增强导航奖励。这使得无人机能够学习一种鲁棒的策略，从而在目标导向的运动和探索性感知之间取得平衡。大量评估表明，与使用固定的、非驱动相机基线相比，我们的策略实现了更安全的飞行，同时也激发了内在的探索行为。

title: SkySim: A ROS2-based Simulation Environment for Natural Language Control of Drone Swarms using Large Language Models
summary: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms offer versatile applications in logistics, agriculture, and surveillance, yet controlling them requires expert knowledge for safety and feasibility. Traditional static methods limit adaptability, while Large Language Models (LLMs) enable natural language control but generate unsafe trajectories due to lacking physical grounding. This paper introduces SkySim, a ROS2-based simulation framework in Gazebo that decouples LLM high-level planning from low-level safety enforcement. Using Gemini 3.5 Pro, SkySim translates user commands (e.g., "Form a circle") into spatial waypoints, informed by real-time drone states. An Artificial Potential Field (APF) safety filter applies minimal adjustments for collision avoidance, kinematic limits, and geo-fencing, ensuring feasible execution at 20 Hz. Experiments with swarms of 3, 10, and 30 Crazyflie drones validate spatial reasoning accuracy (100% across tested geometric primitives), real-time collision prevention, and scalability. SkySim empowers non-experts to iteratively refine behaviors, bridging AI cognition with robotic safety for dynamic environments. Future work targets hardware integration.
tldr: The paper introduces SkySim, a ROS2-based simulation environment that allows natural language control of drone swarms using LLMs, while ensuring safety through an Artificial Potential Field filter, validated with experiments on drone swarms of varying sizes.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SkySim，一个基于ROS2的仿真环境，允许使用LLM对无人机群进行自然语言控制，并通过人工势场滤波器确保安全，并通过对不同尺寸无人机群的实验进行了验证。
summary_zh: 无人机（UAV）集群在物流、农业和监控领域具有广泛的应用前景，但对其进行控制需要专业的知识才能确保安全和可行性。传统的静态方法限制了适应性，而大型语言模型（LLM）虽然支持自然语言控制，但由于缺乏物理基础，会生成不安全的轨迹。本文介绍了一个基于ROS2的SkySim仿真框架，该框架在Gazebo中将LLM的高层规划与低层安全执行解耦。SkySim使用Gemini 3.5 Pro，将用户命令（例如，“形成一个圆”）翻译成空间航路点，并参考实时无人机状态。人工势场（APF）安全滤波器进行最小调整以避免碰撞、限制运动学并进行地理围栏，从而确保以20 Hz的可行执行速度。针对3个、10个和30个Crazyflie无人机集群的实验验证了空间推理的准确性（在测试的几何基元中准确率为100%）、实时碰撞预防和可扩展性。SkySim使非专业人员能够迭代地改进行为，从而将AI认知与机器人安全连接起来，以适应动态环境。未来的工作将致力于硬件集成。

title: SPOT: Spatio-Temporal Obstacle-free Trajectory Planning for UAVs in an Unknown Dynamic Environment
summary: We address the problem of reactive motion planning for quadrotors operating in unknown environments with dynamic obstacles. Our approach leverages a 4-dimensional spatio-temporal planner, integrated with vision-based Safe Flight Corridor (SFC) generation and trajectory optimization. Unlike prior methods that rely on map fusion, our framework is mapless, enabling collision avoidance directly from perception while reducing computational overhead. Dynamic obstacles are detected and tracked using a vision-based object segmentation and tracking pipeline, allowing robust classification of static versus dynamic elements in the scene. To further enhance robustness, we introduce a backup planning module that reactively avoids dynamic obstacles when no direct path to the goal is available, mitigating the risk of collisions during deadlock situations. We validate our method extensively in both simulation and real-world hardware experiments, and benchmark it against state-of-the-art approaches, showing significant advantages for reactive UAV navigation in dynamic, unknown environments.
authors: Thomas J Chackenkulam. Bitla Bhanu Teja
tldr: This paper introduces a mapless spatio-temporal trajectory planner for UAVs operating in unknown dynamic environments, using vision-based perception and a backup planning module for robust collision avoidance. It demonstrates improved performance compared to state-of-the-art methods in simulation and real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种用于在未知动态环境中运行的无人机的无地图时空轨迹规划器，该规划器使用基于视觉的感知和一个备份规划模块来实现鲁棒的避撞。 仿真和实际实验表明，与最先进的方法相比，该方法具有更高的性能。
summary_zh: 我们研究了四旋翼飞行器在具有动态障碍物的未知环境中进行反应式运动规划的问题。 我们的方法利用了一个四维时空规划器，该规划器集成了基于视觉的安全飞行走廊 (SFC) 生成和轨迹优化。 与依赖于地图融合的先前方法不同，我们的框架是无地图的，可以直接从感知中实现避障，同时降低计算开销。 利用基于视觉的对象分割和跟踪流程，检测和跟踪动态障碍物，从而能够对场景中的静态与动态元素进行稳健分类。 为了进一步增强鲁棒性，我们引入了一个备用规划模块，当没有到达目标的直接路径可用时，它可以反应式地避开动态障碍物，从而降低死锁情况下发生碰撞的风险。 我们在仿真和真实硬件实验中对我们的方法进行了广泛的验证，并将其与最先进的方法进行了基准测试，显示了其在动态、未知环境中进行反应式无人机导航的显著优势。

title: Improving Robustness of Vision-Language-Action Models by Restoring Corrupted Visual Inputs
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a dominant paradigm for generalist robotic manipulation, unifying perception and control within a single end-to-end architecture. However, despite their success in controlled environments, reliable real-world deployment is severely hindered by their fragility to visual disturbances. While existing literature extensively addresses physical occlusions caused by scene geometry, a critical mode remains largely unexplored: image corruptions. These sensor-level artifacts, ranging from electronic noise and dead pixels to lens contaminants, directly compromise the integrity of the visual signal prior to interpretation. In this work, we quantify this vulnerability, demonstrating that state-of-the-art VLAs such as $π_{0.5}$ and SmolVLA, suffer catastrophic performance degradation, dropping from 90\% success rates to as low as 2\%, under common signal artifacts. To mitigate this, we introduce the Corruption Restoration Transformer (CRT), a plug-and-play and model-agnostic vision transformer designed to immunize VLA models against sensor disturbances. Leveraging an adversarial training objective, CRT restores clean observations from corrupted inputs without requiring computationally expensive fine-tuning of the underlying model. Extensive experiments across the LIBERO and Meta-World benchmarks demonstrate that CRT effectively recovers lost performance, enabling VLAs to maintain near-baseline success rates, even under severe visual corruption.
tldr: The paper introduces a Corruption Restoration Transformer (CRT) to improve the robustness of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models against image corruptions, a largely unexplored vulnerability affecting their real-world deployment.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种 Corruption Restoration Transformer (CRT)，旨在提高 Vision-Language-Action (VLA) 模型对图像损坏的鲁棒性，这是一个很大程度上未被探索的漏洞，影响了它们在现实世界中的部署。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型已成为通用机器人操作的主导范式，在单一端到端架构中统一感知和控制。然而，尽管它们在受控环境中取得了成功，但由于它们对视觉干扰的脆弱性，可靠的现实世界部署受到严重阻碍。虽然现有文献广泛讨论了由场景几何结构引起的物理遮挡，但一个关键模式仍未得到充分探索：图像损坏。这些传感器级别的伪影，从电子噪声和坏像素到镜头污染物，直接损害了视觉信号在解释前的完整性。在这项工作中，我们量化了这种脆弱性，表明最先进的VLA模型，如$π_{0.5}$和SmolVLA，在常见的信号伪影下遭受灾难性的性能下降，成功率从90%降至低至2%。为了缓解这种情况，我们引入了Corruption Restoration Transformer (CRT)，这是一种即插即用且与模型无关的视觉Transformer，旨在使VLA模型免受传感器干扰的影响。利用对抗性训练目标，CRT从损坏的输入中恢复干净的观测结果，而无需对底层模型进行计算昂贵的微调。在LIBERO和Meta-World基准测试中的大量实验表明，CRT有效地恢复了损失的性能，使VLA能够在严重的视觉损坏下也能保持接近基线的成功率。

title: PolicyFlow: Policy Optimization with Continuous Normalizing Flow in Reinforcement Learning
summary: Among on-policy reinforcement learning algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) demonstrates is widely favored for its simplicity, numerical stability, and strong empirical performance. Standard PPO relies on surrogate objectives defined via importance ratios, which require evaluating policy likelihood that is typically straightforward when the policy is modeled as a Gaussian distribution. However, extending PPO to more expressive, high-capacity policy models such as continuous normalizing flows (CNFs), also known as flow-matching models, is challenging because likelihood evaluation along the full flow trajectory is computationally expensive and often numerically unstable. To resolve this issue, we propose PolicyFlow, a novel on-policy CNF-based reinforcement learning algorithm that integrates expressive CNF policies with PPO-style objectives without requiring likelihood evaluation along the full flow path. PolicyFlow approximates importance ratios using velocity field variations along a simple interpolation path, reducing computational overhead without compromising training stability. To further prevent mode collapse and further encourage diverse behaviors, we propose the Brownian Regularizer, an implicit policy entropy regularizer inspired by Brownian motion, which is conceptually elegant and computationally lightweight. Experiments on diverse tasks across various environments including MultiGoal, PointMaze, IsaacLab and MuJoCo Playground show that PolicyFlow achieves competitive or superior performance compared to PPO using Gaussian policies and flow-based baselines including FPO and DPPO. Notably, results on MultiGoal highlight PolicyFlow's ability to capture richer multimodal action distributions.
tldr: This paper introduces PolicyFlow, a novel on-policy reinforcement learning algorithm using Continuous Normalizing Flows (CNFs) with PPO-style objectives, avoiding full likelihood evaluation. It demonstrates improved performance and the ability to capture richer multimodal action distributions.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了PolicyFlow，一种新颖的on-policy强化学习算法，它使用连续归一化流（CNF）与PPO风格的目标函数，避免了完整的可能性评估。它展示了改进的性能和捕获更丰富的多模态动作分布的能力。
summary_zh: 近端策略优化（PPO）算法因其简洁性、数值稳定性及卓越的实证性能而广受欢迎。标准PPO依赖于通过重要性采样比率定义的替代目标，这需要评估策略似然性，当策略被建模为高斯分布时，通常可以轻松实现。然而，将PPO扩展到更具表现力的高容量策略模型，例如连续归一化流（CNF，也称为流匹配模型）时，面临挑战，因为沿着完整流轨迹的似然性评估计算量大且数值不稳定。为了解决这个问题，我们提出PolicyFlow，一种新颖的基于CNF的在线策略强化学习算法，它将富有表现力的CNF策略与PPO风格的目标相结合，而无需沿着完整的流路径进行似然性评估。PolicyFlow使用沿着简单插值路径的速度场变化来近似重要性比率，从而在不影响训练稳定性的前提下减少计算开销。为了进一步防止模式崩塌并鼓励多样化的行为，我们提出了布朗正则化器，一种受布朗运动启发的隐式策略熵正则化器，它在概念上优雅且计算上轻量。在包括MultiGoal、PointMaze、IsaacLab和MuJoCo Playground在内的各种环境中的多样化任务上的实验表明，与使用高斯策略的PPO和包括FPO和DPPO在内的基于流的基线相比，PolicyFlow实现了有竞争力或更优越的性能。值得注意的是，MultiGoal上的结果突出了PolicyFlow捕获更丰富的多模态动作分布的能力。

title: UniForce: A Unified Latent Force Model for Robot Manipulation with Diverse Tactile Sensors
summary: Force sensing is essential for dexterous robot manipulation, but scaling force-aware policy learning is hindered by the heterogeneity of tactile sensors. Differences in sensing principles (e.g., optical vs. magnetic), form factors, and materials typically require sensor-specific data collection, calibration, and model training, thereby limiting generalisability. We propose UniForce, a novel unified tactile representation learning framework that learns a shared latent force space across diverse tactile sensors. UniForce reduces cross-sensor domain shift by jointly modeling inverse dynamics (image-to-force) and forward dynamics (force-to-image), constrained by force equilibrium and image reconstruction losses to produce force-grounded representations. To avoid reliance on expensive external force/torque (F/T) sensors, we exploit static equilibrium and collect force-paired data via direct sensor--object--sensor interactions, enabling cross-sensor alignment with contact force. The resulting universal tactile encoder can be plugged into downstream force-aware robot manipulation tasks with zero-shot transfer, without retraining or finetuning. Extensive experiments on heterogeneous tactile sensors including GelSight, TacTip, and uSkin, demonstrate consistent improvements in force estimation over prior methods, and enable effective cross-sensor coordination in Vision-Tactile-Language-Action (VTLA) models for a robotic wiping task. Code and datasets will be released.
tldr: UniForce learns a unified latent force representation across diverse tactile sensors by modeling inverse and forward dynamics, enabling zero-shot transfer to downstream force-aware robot manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: UniForce通过建模逆动力学和正动力学，学习跨多种触觉传感器的统一潜在力表示，从而实现零样本迁移到下游的力感知机器人操作任务。
summary_zh: 力感测对于灵巧的机器人操作至关重要，但力感知策略学习的扩展受到触觉传感器异质性的阻碍。传感原理（如光学与磁性）、外形尺寸和材料的差异通常需要针对特定传感器的数据采集、校准和模型训练，从而限制了泛化能力。我们提出了UniForce，一种新颖的统一触觉表征学习框架，它学习一个跨越不同触觉传感器的共享潜在力空间。UniForce通过联合建模逆动力学（图像到力的映射）和正动力学（力到图像的映射），并辅以力平衡和图像重建损失约束，以产生基于力的表征，从而减少了跨传感器域的偏移。为了避免对昂贵的外部力/矩 (F/T) 传感器的依赖，我们利用静态平衡，并通过直接的传感器-物体-传感器交互收集力配对数据，从而实现基于接触力的跨传感器对齐。由此产生的通用触觉编码器无需重新训练或微调，即可通过零样本迁移的方式应用于下游的力感知机器人操作任务。对包括GelSight、TacTip和uSkin在内的各种异构触觉传感器进行的大量实验表明，与先前方法相比，力估计方面的一致改进，并实现了视觉-触觉-语言-动作 (VTLA) 模型在机器人擦拭任务中的有效跨传感器协调。代码和数据集将会发布。

title: KAN We Flow? Advancing Robotic Manipulation with 3D Flow Matching via KAN & RWKV
summary: Diffusion-based visuomotor policies excel at modeling action distributions but are inference-inefficient, since recursively denoising from noise to policy requires many steps and heavy UNet backbones, which hinders deployment on resource-constrained robots. Flow matching alleviates the sampling burden by learning a one-step vector field, yet prior implementations still inherit large UNet-style architectures. In this work, we present KAN-We-Flow, a flow-matching policy that draws on recent advances in Receptance Weighted Key Value (RWKV) and Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN) from vision to build a lightweight and highly expressive backbone for 3D manipulation. Concretely, we introduce an RWKV-KAN block: an RWKV first performs efficient time/channel mixing to propagate task context, and a subsequent GroupKAN layer applies learnable spline-based, groupwise functional mappings to perform feature-wise nonlinear calibration of the action mapping on RWKV outputs. Moreover, we introduce an Action Consistency Regularization (ACR), a lightweight auxiliary loss that enforces alignment between predicted action trajectories and expert demonstrations via Euler extrapolation, providing additional supervision to stabilize training and improve policy precision. Without resorting to large UNets, our design reduces parameters by 86.8\%, maintains fast runtime, and achieves state-of-the-art success rates on Adroit, Meta-World, and DexArt benchmarks. Our project page can be viewed in \href{https://zhihaochen-2003.github.io/KAN-We-Flow.github.io/}{\textcolor{red}{link}}
tldr: The paper presents KAN-We-Flow, a novel flow-matching policy leveraging RWKV and KAN to achieve state-of-the-art manipulation performance with significantly fewer parameters than UNet-based approaches, addressing the inference inefficiency of diffusion models.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了 KAN-We-Flow，一种新颖的流匹配策略，利用 RWKV 和 KAN 以比基于 UNet 的方法少得多的参数实现最先进的操作性能，解决了扩散模型的推理效率低下问题。
summary_zh: 基于扩散的视觉运动策略擅长建模动作分布，但推理效率低下，因为从噪声递归去噪到策略需要很多步骤和繁重的UNet骨干网络，这阻碍了其在资源受限机器人上的部署。流匹配通过学习一步向量场来减轻采样负担，但之前的实现仍然继承了大型UNet风格的架构。在这项工作中，我们提出了KAN-We-Flow，一种流匹配策略，它利用视觉领域的感受加权键值（RWKV）和柯尔莫哥洛夫-阿诺德网络（KAN）的最新进展，为3D操控构建一个轻量级且高表现力的骨干网络。具体来说，我们引入了一个RWKV-KAN块：RWKV首先执行高效的时间/通道混合来传播任务上下文，随后的GroupKAN层应用可学习的基于样条的、分组的功能映射，以对RWKV输出执行动作映射的特征级非线性校准。此外，我们引入了动作一致性正则化（ACR），这是一种轻量级的辅助损失，通过欧拉外推法强制预测的动作轨迹与专家演示之间的一致性，从而提供额外的监督来稳定训练并提高策略精度。在不依赖大型UNet的情况下，我们的设计减少了86.8%的参数，保持了快速的运行时，并在Adroit、Meta-World和DexArt基准测试中实现了最先进的成功率。我们的项目页面可以在\href{https://zhihaochen-2003.github.io/KAN-We-Flow.github.io/}{\textcolor{red}{链接}}中查看。

title: StepNav: Structured Trajectory Priors for Efficient and Multimodal Visual Navigation
summary: Visual navigation is fundamental to autonomous systems, yet generating reliable trajectories in cluttered and uncertain environments remains a core challenge. Recent generative models promise end-to-end synthesis, but their reliance on unstructured noise priors often yields unsafe, inefficient, or unimodal plans that cannot meet real-time requirements. We propose StepNav, a novel framework that bridges this gap by introducing structured, multimodal trajectory priors derived from variational principles. StepNav first learns a geometry-aware success probability field to identify all feasible navigation corridors. These corridors are then used to construct an explicit, multi-modal mixture prior that initializes a conditional flow-matching process. This refinement is formulated as an optimal control problem with explicit smoothness and safety regularization. By replacing unstructured noise with physically-grounded candidates, StepNav generates safer and more efficient plans in significantly fewer steps. Experiments in both simulation and real-world benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements in robustness, efficiency, and safety over state-of-the-art generative planners, advancing reliable trajectory generation for practical autonomous navigation. The code has been released at https://github.com/LuoXubo/StepNav.
tldr: StepNav introduces structured trajectory priors derived from variational principles to improve the safety and efficiency of visual navigation, outperforming state-of-the-art generative planners in both simulation and real-world benchmarks.
tldr_zh: StepNav 引入了从变分原理导出的结构化轨迹先验，以提高视觉导航的安全性和效率，在模拟和真实世界基准测试中均优于最先进的生成规划器。
summary_zh: 视觉导航是自主系统的基础，但在杂乱且不确定的环境中生成可靠轨迹仍然是一个核心挑战。最近的生成模型有望实现端到端综合，但它们对非结构化噪声先验的依赖通常会导致不安全、低效或单峰的规划结果，无法满足实时要求。我们提出 StepNav，一种新型框架，通过引入源于变分原理的结构化、多模态轨迹先验来弥合这一差距。StepNav 首先学习一个几何感知的成功概率场，以识别所有可行的导航通道。然后，这些通道被用于构建一个显式的多模态混合先验，初始化条件流匹配过程。此优化过程被表述为一个具有显式平滑性和安全性正则化的最优控制问题。通过用物理基础的候选方案替换非结构化噪声，StepNav 以明显更少的步骤生成更安全、更高效的规划。在模拟和真实世界基准测试中的实验表明，与最先进的生成规划器相比，StepNav 在鲁棒性、效率和安全性方面均表现出稳定的改进，从而推动了实际自主导航中可靠轨迹生成的进步。代码已在 https://github.com/LuoXubo/StepNav 上发布。

title: Learning Adaptive Cross-Embodiment Visuomotor Policy with Contrastive Prompt Orchestration
summary: Learning adaptive visuomotor policies for embodied agents remains a formidable challenge, particularly when facing cross-embodiment variations such as diverse sensor configurations and dynamic properties. Conventional learning approaches often struggle to separate task-relevant features from domain-specific variations (e.g., lighting, field-of-view, and rotation), leading to poor sample efficiency and catastrophic failure in unseen environments. To bridge this gap, we propose ContrAstive Prompt Orchestration (CAPO), a novel approach for learning visuomotor policies that integrates contrastive prompt learning and adaptive prompt orchestration. For prompt learning, we devise a hybrid contrastive learning strategy that integrates visual, temporal action, and text objectives to establish a pool of learnable prompts, where each prompt induces a visual representation encapsulating fine-grained domain factors. Based on these learned prompts, we introduce an adaptive prompt orchestration mechanism that dynamically aggregates these prompts conditioned on current observations. This enables the agent to adaptively construct optimal state representations by identifying dominant domain factors instantaneously. Consequently, the policy optimization is effectively shielded from irrelevant interference, preventing the common issue of overfitting to source domains. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CAPO significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in sample efficiency and asymptotic performance. Crucially, it exhibits superior zero-shot adaptation across unseen target domains characterized by drastic environmental (e.g., illumination) and physical shifts (e.g., field-of-view and rotation), validating its effectiveness as a viable solution for cross-embodiment visuomotor policy adaptation.
tldr: The paper introduces ContrAstive Prompt Orchestration (CAPO), a novel method for learning adaptive visuomotor policies that leverages contrastive prompt learning and adaptive prompt orchestration for cross-embodiment adaptation, demonstrating improved sample efficiency and zero-shot adaptation performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了对比提示编排(CAPO)，一种新的学习自适应视觉运动策略的方法，它利用对比提示学习和自适应提示编排来实现跨embodiment适应，展示了更高的样本效率和零样本适应性能。
summary_zh: 具身智能体的自适应视觉运动策略学习仍然是一项艰巨的挑战，尤其是在面对跨具身变化时，如多样化的传感器配置和动态属性。 传统的学习方法通常难以将任务相关的特征与特定领域的变化（例如，光照、视野和旋转）分离，导致较差的样本效率和在未见环境中的灾难性失败。 为了弥合这一差距，我们提出对比提示编排 (CAPO)，这是一种用于学习视觉运动策略的新方法，它集成了对比提示学习和自适应提示编排。 对于提示学习，我们设计了一种混合对比学习策略，该策略集成了视觉、时间动作和文本目标，以建立一个可学习的提示池，其中每个提示诱导一个封装了细粒度领域因素的视觉表示。 基于这些学习到的提示，我们引入了一种自适应提示编排机制，该机制根据当前观察动态聚合这些提示。 这使得智能体能够通过即时识别主导领域因素来自适应地构建最优状态表示。 因此，策略优化有效地免受不相关干扰的影响，从而避免了过度拟合源领域的常见问题。 大量实验表明，CAPO 在样本效率和渐近性能方面显著优于最先进的基线。 至关重要的是，它在以剧烈环境（例如，光照）和物理变化（例如，视野和旋转）为特征的未见目标领域中表现出卓越的零样本适应能力，验证了其作为跨具身视觉运动策略适应可行解决方案的有效性。

title: HERMES: A Holistic End-to-End Risk-Aware Multimodal Embodied System with Vision-Language Models for Long-Tail Autonomous Driving
summary: End-to-end autonomous driving models increasingly benefit from large vision--language models for semantic understanding, yet ensuring safe and accurate operation under long-tail conditions remains challenging. These challenges are particularly prominent in long-tail mixed-traffic scenarios, where autonomous vehicles must interact with heterogeneous road users, including human-driven vehicles and vulnerable road users, under complex and uncertain conditions. This paper proposes HERMES, a holistic risk-aware end-to-end multimodal driving framework designed to inject explicit long-tail risk cues into trajectory planning. HERMES employs a foundation-model-assisted annotation pipeline to produce structured Long-Tail Scene Context and Long-Tail Planning Context, capturing hazard-centric cues together with maneuver intent and safety preference, and uses these signals to guide end-to-end planning. HERMES further introduces a Tri-Modal Driving Module that fuses multi-view perception, historical motion cues, and semantic guidance, ensuring risk-aware accurate trajectory planning under long-tail scenarios. Experiments on the real-world long-tail dataset demonstrate that HERMES consistently outperforms representative end-to-end and VLM-driven baselines under long-tail mixed-traffic scenarios. Ablation studies verify the complementary contributions of key components.
tldr: The paper introduces HERMES, an end-to-end autonomous driving framework that leverages vision-language models and a risk-aware approach to improve safety and accuracy in long-tail, mixed-traffic scenarios by incorporating long-tail scene context and a tri-modal driving module for trajectory planning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 HERMES，一个端到端自动驾驶框架，它利用视觉语言模型和风险感知方法，通过整合长尾场景上下文和一个三模态驾驶模块进行轨迹规划，从而提高在长尾混合交通场景中的安全性和准确性。
summary_zh: 端到端自动驾驶模型越来越多地受益于大型视觉-语言模型以进行语义理解，然而，确保其在长尾条件下的安全和准确运行仍然具有挑战性。这些挑战在长尾混合交通场景中尤为突出，在这些场景中，自动驾驶车辆必须在复杂和不确定的条件下与异构道路使用者交互，包括人类驾驶的车辆和弱势道路使用者。本文提出了一种整体的风险感知端到端多模态驾驶框架HERMES，旨在将显式的长尾风险线索注入轨迹规划。HERMES采用基于基础模型的辅助标注流程来生成结构化的长尾场景上下文和长尾规划上下文，捕捉以危害为中心的线索以及机动意图和安全偏好，并使用这些信号来指导端到端规划。HERMES进一步引入了一个三模态驾驶模块，融合多视角感知、历史运动线索和语义引导，确保在长尾场景下进行风险感知的精确轨迹规划。在真实世界长尾数据集上的实验表明，在长尾混合交通场景下，HERMES始终优于具有代表性的端到端和VLM驱动的基线模型。消融研究验证了关键组件的互补贡献。

title: CLAMP: Contrastive Learning for 3D Multi-View Action-Conditioned Robotic Manipulation Pretraining
summary: Leveraging pre-trained 2D image representations in behavior cloning policies has achieved great success and has become a standard approach for robotic manipulation. However, such representations fail to capture the 3D spatial information about objects and scenes that is essential for precise manipulation. In this work, we introduce Contrastive Learning for 3D Multi-View Action-Conditioned Robotic Manipulation Pretraining (CLAMP), a novel 3D pre-training framework that utilizes point clouds and robot actions. From the merged point cloud computed from RGB-D images and camera extrinsics, we re-render multi-view four-channel image observations with depth and 3D coordinates, including dynamic wrist views, to provide clearer views of target objects for high-precision manipulation tasks. The pre-trained encoders learn to associate the 3D geometric and positional information of objects with robot action patterns via contrastive learning on large-scale simulated robot trajectories. During encoder pre-training, we pre-train a Diffusion Policy to initialize the policy weights for fine-tuning, which is essential for improving fine-tuning sample efficiency and performance. After pre-training, we fine-tune the policy on a limited amount of task demonstrations using the learned image and action representations. We demonstrate that this pre-training and fine-tuning design substantially improves learning efficiency and policy performance on unseen tasks. Furthermore, we show that CLAMP outperforms state-of-the-art baselines across six simulated tasks and five real-world tasks.
tldr: CLAMP introduces a 3D pre-training framework for robotic manipulation that uses contrastive learning on point clouds and robot actions to improve fine-tuning sample efficiency and performance, outperforming SOTA baselines in both simulated and real-world tasks.
tldr_zh: CLAMP 提出了一个用于机器人操作的 3D 预训练框架，该框架利用点云和机器人动作上的对比学习来提高微调样本效率和性能，在模拟和现实世界任务中均优于 SOTA 基线。
summary_zh: 在行为克隆策略中利用预训练的2D图像表征已取得巨大成功，并成为机器人操作的标准方法。然而，这种表征无法捕捉物体和场景的3D空间信息，而这对于精确操作至关重要。 在这项工作中，我们引入了用于3D多视角动作条件机器人操作预训练的对比学习(CLAMP)，一种利用点云和机器人动作的新型3D预训练框架。 从RGB-D图像和相机外参计算出的合并点云中，我们重新渲染包含深度和3D坐标的多视角四通道图像观测，包括动态腕部视角，从而为高精度操作任务提供更清晰的目标物体视角。 预训练的编码器通过对大规模模拟机器人轨迹进行对比学习，学习将物体的3D几何和位置信息与机器人动作模式相关联。 在编码器预训练期间，我们预训练一个扩散策略来初始化微调的策略权重，这对于提高微调样本效率和性能至关重要。 预训练后，我们使用学习到的图像和动作表征，在有限的任务演示数据上对策略进行微调。 我们证明了这种预训练和微调设计大大提高了未见任务的学习效率和策略性能。 此外，我们表明CLAMP在六个模拟任务和五个真实世界任务中优于最先进的基线方法。

title: SanD-Planner: Sample-Efficient Diffusion Planner in B-Spline Space for Robust Local Navigation
summary: The challenge of generating reliable local plans has long hindered practical applications in highly cluttered and dynamic environments. Key fundamental bottlenecks include acquiring large-scale expert demonstrations across diverse scenes and improving learning efficiency with limited data. This paper proposes SanD-Planner, a sample-efficient diffusion-based local planner that conducts depth image-based imitation learning within the clamped B-spline space. By operating within this compact space, the proposed algorithm inherently yields smooth outputs with bounded prediction errors over local supports, naturally aligning with receding-horizon execution. Integration of an ESDF-based safety checker with explicit clearance and time-to-completion metrics further reduces the training burden associated with value-function learning for feasibility assessment. Experiments show that training with $500$ episodes (merely $0.25\%$ of the demonstration scale used by the baseline), SanD-Planner achieves state-of-the-art performance on the evaluated open benchmark, attaining success rates of $90.1\%$ in simulated cluttered environments and $72.0\%$ in indoor simulations. The performance is further proven by demonstrating zero-shot transferability to realistic experimentation in both 2D and 3D scenes. The dataset and pre-trained models will also be open-sourced.
tldr: SanD-Planner is a sample-efficient diffusion-based local planner using B-splines and an ESDF-based safety checker. It achieves SOTA performance with significantly less data and demonstrates zero-shot transferability.
tldr_zh: SanD-Planner 是一种基于扩散模型的、样本高效的局部规划器，它使用 B 样条和基于 ESDF 的安全检查器。该方法在少量数据下实现了 SOTA 性能，并展示了零样本迁移能力。
summary_zh: 在高度杂乱和动态的环境中，生成可靠的局部规划一直阻碍着实际应用。主要的基本瓶颈包括：获取跨越不同场景的大规模专家演示数据，以及在有限数据下提高学习效率。本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的具有样本效率的局部规划器SanD-Planner，该规划器在clamp B样条空间内进行基于深度图像的模仿学习。通过在该紧凑空间内运作，所提出的算法固有地产生平滑的输出，并在局部支撑上具有有界的预测误差，自然地与后退视野执行对齐。将基于ESDF的安全检查器与显式的间隙和完成时间指标相结合，进一步降低了价值函数学习对可行性评估的训练负担。实验表明，使用$500$个episode（仅为基线所用演示规模的$0.25\%$）进行训练，SanD-Planner在评估的开放基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，在模拟的杂乱环境中成功率达到$90.1\%$，在室内模拟中达到$72.0\%$。通过展示在2D和3D场景中零样本迁移到真实实验的能力，进一步证明了该算法的性能。该数据集和预训练模型也将开源。

title: Green-VLA: Staged Vision-Language-Action Model for Generalist Robots
summary: We introduce Green-VLA, a staged Vision-Language-Action (VLA) framework for real-world deployment on the Green humanoid robot while maintaining generalization across diverse embodiments. Green-VLA follows a five stage curriculum: (L0) foundational VLMs, (L1) multimodal grounding, (R0) multi-embodiment pretraining, (R1) embodiment-specific adaptation, and (R2) reinforcement-learning (RL) policy alignment. We couple a scalable data-processing pipeline (3,000 hours of demonstrations) with temporal alignment and quality filtering, and use a unified, embodiment-aware action interface enabling a single policy to control humanoids, mobile manipulators, and fixed-base arms. At inference, the VLA controller is enhanced with episode-progress prediction, out-of-distribution detection, and joint-prediction-based guidance to improve safety and precise target selection. Experiments on Simpler BRIDGE WidowX and CALVIN ABC-D, as well as real-robot evaluations, show strong generalization and performance gains from RL alignment in success rate, robustness, and long-horizon efficiency.
tldr: The paper introduces Green-VLA, a staged Vision-Language-Action framework for robots with a curriculum spanning VLMs, grounding, multi-embodiment pretraining, adaptation, and RL alignment, demonstrating generalization across diverse robot embodiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Green-VLA，一个分阶段的视觉-语言-动作框架，用于机器人，包含VLMs、对齐、多形态预训练、适应和RL对齐等阶段，展示了在不同机器人形态上的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 我们提出 Green-VLA，一种分阶段的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）框架，用于在 Green 类人机器人上进行真实世界部署，同时保持在不同具身中的泛化能力。 Green-VLA 遵循五个阶段的课程：（L0）基础视觉语言模型，（L1）多模态对齐，（R0）多具身预训练，（R1）具身特定适应，以及（R2）强化学习（RL）策略对齐。 我们将可扩展的数据处理流程（3000 小时的演示数据）与时间对齐和质量过滤相结合，并使用统一的、具身感知的动作接口，使得单个策略能够控制类人机器人、移动机械臂和固定基座机械臂。 在推理阶段，VLA 控制器通过情景进度预测、分布外检测以及基于联合预测的引导进行增强，以提高安全性和精确目标选择。 在 Simpler BRIDGE WidowX 和 CALVIN ABC-D 上的实验，以及真实机器人评估，表明强化学习对齐在成功率、鲁棒性和长时程效率方面具有强大的泛化能力和性能提升。

title: UniMorphGrasp: Diffusion Model with Morphology-Awareness for Cross-Embodiment Dexterous Grasp Generation
summary: Cross-embodiment dexterous grasping aims to generate stable and diverse grasps for robotic hands with heterogeneous kinematic structures. Existing methods are often tailored to specific hand designs and fail to generalize to unseen hand morphologies outside the training distribution. To address these limitations, we propose \textbf{UniMorphGrasp}, a diffusion-based framework that incorporates hand morphological information into the grasp generation process for unified cross-embodiment grasp synthesis. The proposed approach maps grasps from diverse robotic hands into a unified human-like canonical hand pose representation, providing a common space for learning. Grasp generation is then conditioned on structured representations of hand kinematics, encoded as graphs derived from hand configurations, together with object geometry. In addition, a loss function is introduced that exploits the hierarchical organization of hand kinematics to guide joint-level supervision. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UniMorphGrasp achieves state-of-the-art performance on existing dexterous grasp benchmarks and exhibits strong zero-shot generalization to previously unseen hand structures, enabling scalable and practical cross-embodiment grasp deployment.
tldr: The paper introduces UniMorphGrasp, a diffusion-based framework for cross-embodiment dexterous grasp generation that learns a unified representation conditioned on hand kinematics and object geometry, demonstrating strong zero-shot generalization to unseen hand structures.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了UniMorphGrasp，一个基于扩散模型的跨具身灵巧抓取生成框架，它学习一个统一的表示，该表示以手部运动学和物体几何形状为条件，展示了对未见过的手部结构的强大零样本泛化能力。
summary_zh: 跨具身灵巧抓取旨在为具有异构运动学结构的机械手生成稳定且多样的抓取姿势。现有方法通常针对特定手部设计定制，并且无法泛化到训练分布之外的未见手部形态。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了\textbf{UniMorphGrasp}，一个基于扩散的框架，它将手部形态信息融入到抓取生成过程中，以实现统一的跨具身抓取合成。所提出的方法将来自不同机械手的抓取姿势映射到一个统一的类人规范手部姿势表示中，从而提供了一个通用的学习空间。抓取生成随后以手部运动学的结构化表示为条件，该表示编码为从手部配置导出的图，以及物体几何信息。此外，引入了一个损失函数，该函数利用手部运动学的分层组织结构来指导关节级别的监督。大量实验表明，UniMorphGrasp在现有的灵巧抓取基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，并且对先前未见的手部结构表现出强大的零样本泛化能力，从而能够实现可扩展且实用的跨具身抓取部署。

title: RoDiF: Robust Direct Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Policies with Corrupted Human Feedback
summary: Diffusion policies are a powerful paradigm for robotic control, but fine-tuning them with human preferences is fundamentally challenged by the multi-step structure of the denoising process. To overcome this, we introduce a Unified Markov Decision Process (MDP) formulation that coherently integrates the diffusion denoising chain with environmental dynamics, enabling reward-free Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) for diffusion policies. Building on this formulation, we propose RoDiF (Robust Direct Fine-Tuning), a method that explicitly addresses corrupted human preferences. RoDiF reinterprets the DPO objective through a geometric hypothesis-cutting perspective and employs a conservative cutting strategy to achieve robustness without assuming any specific noise distribution. Extensive experiments on long-horizon manipulation tasks show that RoDiF consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, effectively steering pretrained diffusion policies of diverse architectures to human-preferred modes, while maintaining strong performance even under 30% corrupted preference labels.
tldr: The paper introduces RoDiF, a robust direct preference optimization method for fine-tuning diffusion policies in robotics using potentially corrupted human feedback, achieving state-of-the-art performance in long-horizon manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了RoDiF，一种鲁棒的直接偏好优化方法，用于在机器人技术中微调扩散策略，使用可能被破坏的人类反馈，并在长视野操作任务中实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 扩散策略是机器人控制的一种强大范式，但通过人类偏好对其进行微调，从根本上受到去噪过程多步结构的挑战。为了克服这个问题，我们引入了一种统一的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）公式，该公式将扩散去噪链与环境动力学连贯地整合在一起，从而为扩散策略实现免奖励的直接偏好优化（DPO）。在此公式的基础上，我们提出了一种名为RoDiF（稳健直接微调）的方法，该方法明确地解决了受损的人类偏好问题。RoDiF通过几何假设切割视角重新解释了DPO目标，并采用保守的切割策略来实现稳健性，而无需假设任何特定的噪声分布。在长期操纵任务上的大量实验表明，RoDiF始终优于最先进的基线，有效地引导具有不同架构的预训练扩散策略进入人类偏好的模式，同时即使在30%的偏好标签受损的情况下也能保持强大的性能。

title: Safe Stochastic Explorer: Enabling Safe Goal Driven Exploration in Stochastic Environments and Safe Interaction with Unknown Objects
summary: Autonomous robots operating in unstructured, safety-critical environments, from planetary exploration to warehouses and homes, must learn to safely navigate and interact with their surroundings despite limited prior knowledge. Current methods for safe control, such as Hamilton-Jacobi Reachability and Control Barrier Functions, assume known system dynamics. Meanwhile existing safe exploration techniques often fail to account for the unavoidable stochasticity inherent when operating in unknown real world environments, such as an exploratory rover skidding over an unseen surface or a household robot pushing around unmapped objects in a pantry. To address this critical gap, we propose Safe Stochastic Explorer (S.S.Explorer) a novel framework for safe, goal-driven exploration under stochastic dynamics. Our approach strategically balances safety and information gathering to reduce uncertainty about safety in the unknown environment. We employ Gaussian Processes to learn the unknown safety function online, leveraging their predictive uncertainty to guide information-gathering actions and provide probabilistic bounds on safety violations. We first present our method for discrete state space environments and then introduce a scalable relaxation to effectively extend this approach to continuous state spaces. Finally we demonstrate how this framework can be naturally applied to ensure safe physical interaction with multiple unknown objects. Extensive validation in simulation and demonstrative hardware experiments showcase the efficacy of our method, representing a step forward toward enabling reliable widespread robot autonomy in complex, uncertain environments.
tldr: The paper introduces Safe Stochastic Explorer (S.S.Explorer), a novel framework for safe, goal-driven exploration under stochastic dynamics using Gaussian Processes to learn the unknown safety function online. It balances safety and information gathering to reduce uncertainty and is validated in simulation and hardware experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了安全随机探索器（S.S.Explorer），这是一个新的框架，用于在随机动态下进行安全、目标驱动的探索，使用高斯过程在线学习未知的安全函数。它平衡了安全性和信息收集，以减少不确定性，并在模拟和硬件实验中进行了验证。
summary_zh: 在非结构化、安全攸关的环境中运行的自主机器人，无论是行星探测还是仓库和家庭，都必须在先验知识有限的情况下学习安全地导航并与周围环境交互。目前的安全控制方法，如Hamilton-Jacobi可达性和控制屏障函数，都假设系统动力学已知。同时，现有的安全探索技术通常无法考虑到在未知的真实世界环境中运行时不可避免的随机性，例如探索车在未见表面上打滑，或者家用机器人在食品室里推动未绘制地图的物体。为了解决这一关键差距，我们提出了一种名为安全随机探索器（S.S.Explorer）的新型框架，用于在随机动力学下进行安全的、目标驱动的探索。我们的方法策略性地平衡了安全性和信息收集，以减少对未知环境中安全性的不确定性。我们采用高斯过程在线学习未知的安全函数，利用其预测不确定性来指导信息收集行为，并提供安全违规的概率界限。我们首先介绍了我们针对离散状态空间环境的方法，然后引入了一种可扩展的松弛算法，以有效地将这种方法扩展到连续状态空间。最后，我们展示了如何自然地应用该框架，以确保与多个未知对象进行安全的物理交互。大量的仿真验证和演示性的硬件实验展示了我们方法的有效性，代表着在复杂、不确定环境中实现可靠的广泛机器人自主性方面向前迈进了一步。

title: Sample Efficient Active Algorithms for Offline Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) enables policy learning from static data but often suffers from poor coverage of the state-action space and distributional shift problems. This problem can be addressed by allowing limited online interactions to selectively refine uncertain regions of the learned value function, which is referred to as Active Reinforcement Learning (ActiveRL). While there has been good empirical success, no theoretical analysis is available in the literature. We fill this gap by developing a rigorous sample-complexity analysis of ActiveRL through the lens of Gaussian Process (GP) uncertainty modeling. In this respect, we propose an algorithm and using GP concentration inequalities and information-gain bounds, we derive high-probability guarantees showing that an $ε$-optimal policy can be learned with ${\mathcal{O}}(1/ε^2)$ active transitions, improving upon the $Ω(1/ε^2(1-γ)^4)$ rate of purely offline methods. Our results reveal that ActiveRL achieves near-optimal information efficiency, that is, guided uncertainty reduction leads to accelerated value-function convergence with minimal online data. Our analysis builds on GP concentration inequalities and information-gain bounds, bridging Bayesian nonparametric regression and reinforcement learning theories. We conduct several experiments to validate the algorithm and theoretical findings.
tldr: This paper provides a theoretical analysis and algorithm for Active Reinforcement Learning (ActiveRL) using Gaussian Process uncertainty modeling, achieving improved sample efficiency compared to purely offline RL methods. They provide theoretical guarantees and empirical validation.
tldr_zh: 本文使用高斯过程不确定性建模，为主动强化学习 (ActiveRL) 提供了理论分析和算法，与纯离线强化学习方法相比，提高了样本效率。 他们提供了理论保证和实验验证。
summary_zh: 离线强化学习(RL)允许从静态数据中学习策略，但往往遭受状态-动作空间覆盖率不足和分布偏移问题的困扰。这个问题可以通过允许有限的在线交互来选择性地改进学习到的值函数的不确定区域来解决，这种方法被称为主动强化学习(ActiveRL)。虽然已经取得了良好的经验性成功，但文献中没有相应的理论分析。我们弥补了这个空白，从高斯过程(GP)不确定性建模的角度，对ActiveRL进行了严格的样本复杂度分析。在这方面，我们提出了一种算法，并利用GP集中不等式和信息增益界限，推导出高概率保证，表明可以通过${\mathcal{O}}(1/ε^2)$次主动转移学习到一个$ε$-最优策略，优于纯离线方法的$Ω(1/ε^2(1-γ)^4)$速率。我们的结果表明，ActiveRL实现了近乎最佳的信息效率，即，有指导的不确定性减少可以以最小的在线数据实现加速的值函数收敛。 我们的分析建立在GP集中不等式和信息增益界限的基础上，桥接了贝叶斯非参数回归和强化学习理论。我们进行了多个实验来验证该算法和理论发现。

title: Workflow-R1: Group Sub-sequence Policy Optimization for Multi-turn Workflow Construction
summary: The rapid evolution of agentic workflows has demonstrated strong performance of LLM-based agents in addressing complex reasoning tasks. However, existing workflow optimization methods typically formulate workflow synthesis as a static, one-shot code-centric generation problem. This paradigm imposes excessive constraints on the model's coding capabilities and restricts the flexibility required for dynamic problem-solving. In this paper, we present Workflow-R1, a framework that reformulates workflow construction as a multi-turn, natural language-based sequential decision-making process. To resolve the optimization granularity mismatch inherent in such multi-turn interactions, we introduce Group Sub-sequence Policy Optimization (GSsPO). While explicitly tailored to align with the interleaved Think-Action dynamics of agentic reasoning, GSsPO fundamentally functions as a structure-aware RL algorithm generalizable to a broad class of multi-turn agentic sequential decision-making tasks. By recalibrating the optimization unit to the composite sub-sequence, specifically the atomic Think-Action cycle, it aligns gradient updates with the semantic boundaries of these interactions, ensuring robust learning in complex multi-turn reasoning tasks. Through extensive experiments on multiple QA benchmarks, Workflow-R1 outperforms competitive baselines, validating GSsPO as a generalized solution for sequential reasoning and establishing Workflow-R1 as a promising new paradigm for automated workflow optimization.
tldr: The paper introduces Workflow-R1, a framework that uses Group Sub-sequence Policy Optimization (GSsPO), a structure-aware RL algorithm, to improve multi-turn workflow construction by optimizing Think-Action cycles in LLM-based agents, demonstrating significant performance improvements on QA benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Workflow-R1，一个使用群组子序列策略优化（GSsPO）的框架，该框架是一种结构感知的强化学习算法，通过优化基于LLM的agent的思考-行动周期来改进多轮工作流构建，并在QA基准测试中表现出显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型的智能体工作流在解决复杂推理任务方面展现出强大的性能，智能体工作流的快速发展证明了这一点。然而，现有的工作流优化方法通常将工作流合成形式化为静态、一次性的以代码为中心的生成问题。这种范式对模型的编码能力施加了过度的约束，并限制了动态解决问题所需的灵活性。在本文中，我们提出了Workflow-R1，一个将工作流构建重构为多轮、基于自然语言的序贯决策过程的框架。为了解决这种多轮交互中固有的优化粒度不匹配问题，我们引入了组子序列策略优化（GSsPO）。GSsPO虽然专门为与智能体推理中交错的思考-行动动态对齐而定制，但从根本上来说，它是一种结构感知的强化学习算法，能够推广到广泛的多轮智能体序贯决策任务。通过将优化单元重新校准为复合子序列，特别是原子思考-行动循环，它使梯度更新与这些交互的语义边界对齐，从而确保在复杂的多轮推理任务中进行稳健的学习。通过在多个问答基准上进行的大量实验，Workflow-R1优于具有竞争力的基线，验证了GSsPO作为序贯推理的通用解决方案，并将Workflow-R1确立为自动化工作流优化的一个有前景的新范式。

title: SetPO: Set-Level Policy Optimization for Diversity-Preserving LLM Reasoning
summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has shown notable effectiveness in enhancing large language models (LLMs) reasoning performance, especially in mathematics tasks. However, such improvements often come with reduced outcome diversity, where the model concentrates probability mass on a narrow set of solutions. Motivated by diminishing-returns principles, we introduce a set level diversity objective defined over sampled trajectories using kernelized similarity. Our approach derives a leave-one-out marginal contribution for each sampled trajectory and integrates this objective as a plug-in advantage shaping term for policy optimization. We further investigate the contribution of a single trajectory to language model diversity within a distribution perturbation framework. This analysis theoretically confirms a monotonicity property, proving that rarer trajectories yield consistently higher marginal contributions to the global diversity. Extensive experiments across a range of model scales demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithm, consistently outperforming strong baselines in both Pass@1 and Pass@K across various benchmarks.
tldr: This paper introduces SetPO, a reinforcement learning method that enhances LLM reasoning performance while preserving outcome diversity by using a set-level diversity objective and marginal contribution-based advantage shaping.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了SetPO，一种强化学习方法，通过使用集合级别的多样性目标和基于边际贡献的优势塑造来增强LLM的推理性能，同时保持结果的多样性。
summary_zh: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习已在提升大型语言模型(LLMs)的推理性能，特别是数学任务方面，展现出显著的有效性。然而，此类改进往往伴随着结果多样性的降低，即模型将概率质量集中在一小部分解决方案上。受收益递减原则的启发，我们引入了一个集合级别的多样性目标，该目标使用核化的相似性定义在采样的轨迹上。我们的方法为每个采样的轨迹推导出一种留一法边际贡献，并将此目标作为策略优化的插件式优势塑造项进行集成。我们进一步在一个分布扰动框架内研究了单个轨迹对语言模型多样性的贡献。此分析从理论上证实了一种单调性属性，证明了稀有轨迹对全局多样性的边际贡献始终更高。在各种模型规模上进行的大量实验表明了我们提出的算法的有效性，在各种基准测试中的Pass@1和Pass@K指标上均持续优于强大的基线方法。

title: Residual Decoding: Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models via History-Aware Residual Guidance
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) can reason effectively from image-text inputs and perform well in various multimodal tasks. Despite this success, they are affected by language priors and often produce hallucinations. Hallucinations denote generated content that is grammatically and syntactically coherent, yet bears no match or direct relevance to actual visual input. To address this problem, we propose Residual Decoding (ResDec). It is a novel training-free method that uses historical information to aid decoding. The method relies on the internal implicit reasoning mechanism and token logits evolution mechanism of LVLMs to correct biases. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ResDec effectively suppresses hallucinations induced by language priors, significantly improves visual grounding, and reduces object hallucinations. In addition to mitigating hallucinations, ResDec also performs exceptionally well on comprehensive LVLM benchmarks, highlighting its broad applicability.
tldr: The paper introduces Residual Decoding (ResDec), a training-free method to mitigate hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) by leveraging historical information and the internal reasoning mechanisms of LVLMs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为残差解码 (ResDec) 的免训练方法，旨在通过利用历史信息和 LVLM 的内部推理机制来缓解大型视觉语言模型 (LVLM) 中的幻觉问题。
summary_zh: 大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）可以有效地从图像-文本输入进行推理，并在各种多模态任务中表现出色。 尽管取得了成功，但它们仍然受到语言先验的影响，并且经常产生幻觉。 幻觉是指生成的在语法和句法上连贯的内容，但与实际视觉输入不匹配或没有直接相关性。 为了解决这个问题，我们提出了残差解码（ResDec）。 这是一种新颖的免训练方法，它利用历史信息来辅助解码。 该方法依赖于LVLMs的内在隐式推理机制和token logits演化机制来纠正偏差。 大量实验表明，ResDec有效地抑制了由语言先验引起的幻觉，显著提高了视觉基础（visual grounding），并减少了对象幻觉。 除了减轻幻觉之外，ResDec在全面的LVLM基准测试中也表现出色，突显了其广泛的适用性。

title: Discovering Process-Outcome Credit in Multi-Step LLM Reasoning
summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) serves as a potent paradigm for enhancing reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs), yet standard outcome-based approaches often suffer from reward sparsity and inefficient credit assignment. In this paper, we propose a novel framework designed to provide continuous reward signals, which introduces a Step-wise Marginal Information Gain (MIG) mechanism that quantifies the intrinsic value of reasoning steps against a Monotonic Historical Watermark, effectively filtering out training noise. To ensure disentangled credit distribution, we implement a Decoupled Masking Strategy, applying process-oriented rewards specifically to the chain-of-thought (CoT) and outcome-oriented rewards to the full completion. Additionally, we incorporate a Dual-Gated SFT objective to stabilize training with high-quality structural and factual signals. Extensive experiments across textual and multi-modal benchmarks (e.g., MATH, Super-CLEVR) demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms baselines such as GRPO in both sample efficiency and final accuracy. Furthermore, our model exhibits superior out-of-distribution robustness, demonstrating promising zero-shot transfer capabilities to unseen and challenging reasoning tasks.
tldr: This paper introduces a novel Reinforcement Learning framework for LLMs that enhances reasoning by using step-wise rewards based on information gain and decoupled masking strategies, demonstrating improved sample efficiency, accuracy, and out-of-distribution robustness on reasoning tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的LLM强化学习框架，通过使用基于信息增益的逐步奖励和解耦的掩蔽策略来增强推理能力，并在推理任务上展示了更高的样本效率、准确性和分布外鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 强化学习（RL）是一种增强大型语言模型（LLMs）推理能力的强大范式，但标准的基于结果的方法通常会受到奖励稀疏性和低效信用分配的影响。在本文中，我们提出了一种新型框架，旨在提供连续的奖励信号，引入了一个逐步边际信息增益（MIG）机制，该机制可针对单调历史水印量化推理步骤的内在价值，有效过滤掉训练噪声。为了确保解耦的信用分配，我们实施了一种解耦掩码策略，将面向过程的奖励专门应用于思维链（CoT），将面向结果的奖励应用于完整的补全。此外，我们还结合了双门控SFT目标，以利用高质量的结构和事实信号来稳定训练。在文本和多模态基准测试（例如，MATH、Super-CLEVR）上的大量实验表明，我们的方法在样本效率和最终准确性方面均持续优于诸如GRPO之类的基线。此外，我们的模型表现出卓越的分布外鲁棒性，展示了对未见过且具有挑战性的推理任务的有希望的零样本迁移能力。

title: Reasoning and Tool-use Compete in Agentic RL:From Quantifying Interference to Disentangled Tuning
summary: Agentic Reinforcement Learning (ARL) focuses on training large language models (LLMs) to interleave reasoning with external tool execution to solve complex tasks. Most existing ARL methods train a single shared model parameters to support both reasoning and tool use behaviors, implicitly assuming that joint training leads to improved overall agent performance. Despite its widespread adoption, this assumption has rarely been examined empirically. In this paper, we systematically investigate this assumption by introducing a Linear Effect Attribution System(LEAS), which provides quantitative evidence of interference between reasoning and tool-use behaviors. Through an in-depth analysis, we show that these two capabilities often induce misaligned gradient directions, leading to training interference that undermines the effectiveness of joint optimization and challenges the prevailing ARL paradigm. To address this issue, we propose Disentangled Action Reasoning Tuning(DART), a simple and efficient framework that explicitly decouples parameter updates for reasoning and tool-use via separate low-rank adaptation modules. Experimental results show that DART consistently outperforms baseline methods with averaged 6.35 percent improvements and achieves performance comparable to multi-agent systems that explicitly separate tool-use and reasoning using a single model.
tldr: This paper identifies interference between reasoning and tool-use in Agentic RL, proposing a disentangled tuning method (DART) to improve performance. DART achieves comparable results to multi-agent systems with a single model.
tldr_zh: 本文发现了Agentic RL中推理和工具使用之间的干扰，并提出了一种解耦调优方法（DART）来提高性能。DART使用单个模型实现了与多智能体系统相当的结果。
summary_zh: 自主强化学习（ARL）侧重于训练大型语言模型（LLMs），使其能够将推理与外部工具执行相穿插，以解决复杂任务。大多数现有的 ARL 方法训练单个共享模型参数，以支持推理和工具使用行为，隐含地假设联合训练可以提高整体Agent性能。尽管这种假设被广泛采用，但很少有经验对其进行检验。在本文中，我们通过引入线性效应归因系统（LEAS）来系统地研究这一假设，该系统为推理和工具使用行为之间的干扰提供了定量证据。通过深入分析，我们表明这两种能力通常会引起未对齐的梯度方向，从而导致训练干扰，进而破坏联合优化的有效性，并挑战了当前流行的 ARL 范式。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了分离式行动推理微调（DART），这是一个简单而高效的框架，通过单独的低秩自适应模块，显式地解耦推理和工具使用参数的更新。实验结果表明，DART始终优于基线方法，平均提高了6.35个百分点，并获得了与多Agent系统相当的性能，后者使用单个模型显式地分离工具使用和推理。

title: DISPO: Enhancing Training Efficiency and Stability in Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Model Mathematical Reasoning
summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has emerged as a promising paradigm for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models particularly in mathematics. Current approaches in this domain present a clear trade-off: PPO-style methods (e.g., GRPO/DAPO) offer training stability but exhibit slow learning trajectories due to their trust-region constraints on policy updates, while REINFORCE-style approaches (e.g., CISPO) demonstrate improved learning efficiency but suffer from performance instability as they clip importance sampling weights while still permitting non-zero gradients outside the trust-region. To address these limitations, we introduce DISPO, a simple yet effective REINFORCE-style algorithm that decouples the up-clipping and down-clipping of importance sampling weights for correct and incorrect responses, yielding four controllable policy update regimes. Through targeted ablations, we uncover how each regime impacts training: for correct responses, weights >1 increase the average token entropy (i.e., exploration) while weights <1 decrease it (i.e., distillation) -- both beneficial but causing gradual performance degradation when excessive. For incorrect responses, overly restrictive clipping triggers sudden performance collapse through repetitive outputs (when weights >1) or vanishing response lengths (when weights <1). By separately tuning these four clipping parameters, DISPO maintains the exploration-distillation balance while preventing catastrophic failures, achieving 61.04% on AIME'24 (vs. 55.42% CISPO and 50.21% DAPO) with similar gains across various benchmarks and models.
tldr: The paper introduces DISPO, a REINFORCE-style reinforcement learning algorithm for LLMs that enhances mathematical reasoning. DISPO decouples clipping of importance sampling weights for correct/incorrect responses, achieving better performance and stability compared to existing methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DISPO，一种用于LLM的REINFORCE风格的强化学习算法，旨在提升数学推理能力。DISPO将正确/错误的响应的重要性采样权重剪裁解耦，与现有方法相比，实现了更好的性能和稳定性。
summary_zh: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习已经成为增强大型语言模型推理能力，尤其是在数学方面的有前景的范式。目前该领域的方法呈现出明显的权衡：PPO 类方法（例如，GRPO/DAPO）提供训练稳定性，但由于其对策略更新的置信域约束，表现出缓慢的学习轨迹，而 REINFORCE 类方法（例如，CISPO）表现出更高的学习效率，但由于其在置信域之外仍然允许非零梯度的情况下剪裁重要性采样权重，因此存在性能不稳定问题。为了解决这些局限性，我们引入了 DISPO，这是一种简单而有效的 REINFORCE 类算法，它将正确和不正确响应的重要性采样权重的向上剪裁和向下剪裁解耦，从而产生四个可控的策略更新机制。通过有针对性的消融实验，我们揭示了每种机制如何影响训练：对于正确的响应，权重 >1 会增加平均 token 熵（即，探索），而权重 <1 会降低它（即，提炼）——两者都有益，但过度时会导致性能逐步下降。对于不正确的响应，过于严格的剪裁会通过重复输出（当权重 >1 时）或响应长度消失（当权重 <1 时）触发突然的性能崩溃。通过分别调整这四个剪裁参数，DISPO 保持了探索-提炼的平衡，同时防止了灾难性失败，在 AIME'24 上达到了 61.04% 的准确率（相比之下，CISPO 为 55.42%，DAPO 为 50.21%），并在各种基准测试和模型中获得了类似的提升。

title: MCP-Atlas: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Tool-Use Competency with Real MCP Servers
summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is rapidly becoming the standard interface for Large Language Models (LLMs) to discover and invoke external tools. However, existing evaluations often fail to capture the complexity of real-world scenarios, relying on restricted toolsets, simplistic workflows, or subjective LLM-as-a-judge metrics. We introduce MCP-Atlas, a large-scale benchmark for evaluating tool-use competency, comprising 36 real MCP servers and 220 tools. It includes 1,000 tasks designed to assess tool-use competency in realistic, multi-step workflows. Tasks use natural language prompts that avoid naming specific tools or servers, requiring agents to identify and orchestrate 3-6 tool calls across multiple servers. We score tasks using a claims-based rubric that awards partial credit based on the factual claims satisfied in the model's final answer, complemented by internal diagnostics on tool discovery, parameterization, syntax, error recovery, and efficiency. Evaluation results on frontier models reveal that top models achieve pass rates exceeding 50%, with primary failures arising from inadequate tool usage and task understanding. We release the task schema, containerized harness, and a 500-task public subset of the benchmark dataset to facilitate reproducible comparisons and advance the development of robust, tool-augmented agents.
tldr: The paper introduces MCP-Atlas, a large-scale benchmark to evaluate tool-use competency for LLMs in realistic, multi-step workflows, revealing that even top models struggle with adequate tool usage and task understanding.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 MCP-Atlas，一个大规模基准测试，用于评估 LLM 在真实、多步骤工作流程中的工具使用能力，结果表明，即使是顶级模型也难以充分利用工具并理解任务。
summary_zh: 模型上下文协议（MCP）正迅速成为大型语言模型（LLM）发现并调用外部工具的标准接口。然而，现有的评估往往无法捕捉真实世界场景的复杂性，而是依赖于受限的工具集、简化的工作流程或主观的LLM评判指标。我们推出了MCP-Atlas，一个用于评估工具使用能力的大规模基准测试，包含36个真实的MCP服务器和220个工具。它包括1000个任务，旨在评估在实际、多步骤工作流程中的工具使用能力。任务使用避免提及特定工具或服务器的自然语言提示，要求智能体识别并在多个服务器上协调3-6个工具调用。我们使用基于断言的评分标准对任务打分，根据模型最终答案中满足的事实断言给予部分分数，并辅以内部分析，包括工具发现、参数化、语法、错误恢复和效率。对前沿模型的评估结果表明，顶级模型实现了超过50%的通过率，主要的失败原因是不充分的工具使用和任务理解。我们发布了任务模式、容器化框架以及基准数据集的500个任务的公共子集，以促进可复现的比较并推进鲁棒的、工具增强型智能体的开发。

title: EffGen: Enabling Small Language Models as Capable Autonomous Agents
summary: Most existing language model agentic systems today are built and optimized for large language models (e.g., GPT, Claude, Gemini) via API calls. While powerful, this approach faces several limitations including high token costs and privacy concerns for sensitive applications. We introduce effGen, an open-source agentic framework optimized for small language models (SLMs) that enables effective, efficient, and secure local deployment (pip install effgen). effGen makes four major contributions: (1) Enhanced tool-calling with prompt optimization that compresses contexts by 70-80% while preserving task semantics, (2) Intelligent task decomposition that breaks complex queries into parallel or sequential subtasks based on dependencies, (3) Complexity-based routing using five factors to make smart pre-execution decisions, and (4) Unified memory system combining short-term, long-term, and vector-based storage. Additionally, effGen unifies multiple agent protocols (MCP, A2A, ACP) for cross-protocol communication. Results on 13 benchmarks show effGen outperforms LangChain, AutoGen, and Smolagents with higher success rates, faster execution, and lower memory. Our results reveal that prompt optimization and complexity routing have complementary scaling behavior: optimization benefits SLMs more (11.2% gain at 1.5B vs 2.4% at 32B), while routing benefits large models more (3.6% at 1.5B vs 7.9% at 32B), providing consistent gains across all scales when combined. effGen (https://effgen.org/) is released under the MIT License, ensuring broad accessibility for research and commercial use. Our framework code is publicly available at https://github.com/ctrl-gaurav/effGen.
tldr: The paper introduces effGen, an open-source agentic framework optimized for small language models, featuring enhanced tool-calling, intelligent task decomposition, complexity-based routing, and a unified memory system, outperforming existing frameworks on multiple benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为effGen的开源智能体框架，该框架针对小型语言模型进行了优化，具有增强的工具调用、智能任务分解、基于复杂性的路由和统一的内存系统，在多个基准测试中优于现有框架。
summary_zh: 目前大多数现有语言模型代理系统都是通过 API 调用，针对大型语言模型（例如 GPT、Claude、Gemini）构建和优化的。虽然这种方法功能强大，但面临着几个限制，包括高昂的 token 成本和敏感应用中的隐私问题。我们推出 effGen，一个开源代理框架，专门针对小型语言模型（SLM）优化，可实现有效、高效且安全的本地部署（pip install effgen）。effGen 主要有四点贡献：（1）通过提示优化增强了工具调用功能，能够在保留任务语义的同时压缩 70-80% 的上下文，（2）智能任务分解，基于依赖关系将复杂查询分解为并行或顺序子任务，（3）基于复杂度的路由，使用五个因素进行智能的预执行决策，以及（4）统一的记忆系统，结合了短期、长期和基于向量的存储。此外，effGen 统一了多种代理协议（MCP、A2A、ACP），以实现跨协议通信。在 13 个基准测试上的结果表明，effGen 在成功率更高、执行速度更快、内存占用更少的情况下，优于 LangChain、AutoGen 和 Smolagents。我们的结果表明，提示优化和复杂度路由具有互补的扩展行为：优化更有利于 SLM（1.5B 模型提升 11.2% vs 32B 模型提升 2.4%），而路由更有利于大型模型（1.5B 模型提升 3.6% vs 32B 模型提升 7.9%），当两者结合使用时，可以在所有规模的模型上提供持续的收益。effGen (https://effgen.org/) 在 MIT 许可下发布，确保了研究和商业用途的广泛可及性。我们的框架代码可在 https://github.com/ctrl-gaurav/effGen 公开获取。

title: What Does Vision Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Really Learn? Disentangling Tool-Induced and Intrinsic Effects for Crop-and-Zoom
summary: Vision tool-use reinforcement learning (RL) can equip vision-language models with visual operators such as crop-and-zoom and achieves strong performance gains, yet it remains unclear whether these gains are driven by improvements in tool use or evolving intrinsic capabilities.We introduce MED (Measure-Explain-Diagnose), a coarse-to-fine framework that disentangles intrinsic capability changes from tool-induced effects, decomposes the tool-induced performance difference into gain and harm terms, and probes the mechanisms driving their evolution. Across checkpoint-level analyses on two VLMs with different tool priors and six benchmarks, we find that improvements are dominated by intrinsic learning, while tool-use RL mainly reduces tool-induced harm (e.g., fewer call-induced errors and weaker tool schema interference) and yields limited progress in tool-based correction of intrinsic failures. Overall, current vision tool-use RL learns to coexist safely with tools rather than master them.
tldr: This paper investigates vision tool-use reinforcement learning, finding that performance gains are primarily due to intrinsic learning rather than tool mastery, with RL mainly mitigating tool-induced harm.
tldr_zh: 该论文研究了视觉工具使用的强化学习，发现性能提升主要归功于内在学习，而非工具掌握，强化学习主要用于减轻工具引入的危害。
summary_zh: 视觉工具使用强化学习 (RL) 能够赋予视觉-语言模型裁剪缩放等视觉操作算子，并实现显著的性能提升，但这些提升是源于工具使用的改进还是内在能力的发展，仍不明确。我们提出MED（测量-解释-诊断），一个由粗到精的框架，将内在能力的变化与工具引入的效应解耦，将工具引入的性能差异分解为增益项和损害项，并探究驱动它们演化的机制。通过对具有不同工具先验的两个视觉-语言模型在六个基准测试上的检查点级别分析，我们发现性能的提升主要由内在学习驱动，而工具使用强化学习主要减少了工具引入的损害 (例如，更少的调用导致的错误和更弱的工具模式干扰)，并且在基于工具对内在缺陷的纠正方面进展有限。总体而言，当前的视觉工具使用强化学习学习的是与工具安全共存，而不是掌握它们。

title: From Videos to Conversations: Egocentric Instructions for Task Assistance
summary: Many everyday tasks, ranging from appliance repair and cooking to car maintenance, require expert knowledge, particularly for complex, multi-step procedures. Despite growing interest in AI agents for augmented reality (AR) assistance, progress remains limited by the scarcity of large-scale multimodal conversational datasets grounded in real-world task execution, in part due to the cost and logistical complexity of human-assisted data collection. In this paper, we present a framework to automatically transform single person instructional videos into two-person multimodal task-guidance conversations. Our fully automatic pipeline, based on large language models, provides a scalable and cost efficient alternative to traditional data collection approaches. Using this framework, we introduce HowToDIV, a multimodal dataset comprising 507 conversations, 6,636 question answer pairs, and 24 hours of video spanning multiple domains. Each session consists of a multi-turn expert-novice interaction. Finally, we report baseline results using Gemma 3 and Qwen 2.5 on HowToDIV, providing an initial benchmark for multimodal procedural task assistance.
tldr: The paper introduces a framework for automatically generating multimodal task-guidance conversations from instructional videos, creating a new dataset (HowToDIV) for training AI agents to assist with real-world tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个自动从教学视频生成多模态任务指导对话的框架，并创建了一个新的数据集(HowToDIV)，用于训练AI智能体来协助完成真实世界的任务。
summary_zh: 许多日常任务，从电器维修、烹饪到汽车保养，都需要专业知识，特别是对于复杂、多步骤的流程。尽管人们对用于增强现实(AR)辅助的AI代理越来越感兴趣，但由于缺乏基于真实世界任务执行的大规模多模态对话数据集，进展仍然有限，部分原因是人工辅助数据收集的成本和后勤复杂性。本文中，我们提出了一种框架，可以自动将单人指导视频转换为双人多模态任务指导对话。我们完全自动化的流程基于大型语言模型，为传统数据收集方法提供了一种可扩展且经济高效的替代方案。利用该框架，我们推出了HowToDIV，一个包含507个对话、6636个问答对和24小时视频的多模态数据集，涵盖多个领域。每个会话都包含一个多轮的专家-新手交互。最后，我们报告了在HowToDIV上使用Gemma 3和Qwen 2.5的基线结果，为多模态程序性任务辅助提供了一个初步基准。

title: LRAgent: Efficient KV Cache Sharing for Multi-LoRA LLM Agents
summary: Role specialization in multi-LLM agent systems is often realized via multi-LoRA, where agents share a pretrained backbone and differ only through lightweight adapters. Despite sharing base model weights, each agent independently builds and stores its own KV cache for the same long, tool-augmented trajectories, incurring substantial memory and compute overhead. Existing KV cache sharing methods largely overlook this multi-LoRA setting. We observe that, across agents, cache differences are dominated by adapter outputs, while activations from the shared pretrained backbone remain highly similar. Based on this observation, we propose LRAgent, a KV cache sharing framework for multi-LoRA agents that decomposes the cache into a shared base component from the pretrained weights and an adapter-dependent component from LoRA weights. LRAgent reduces memory overhead by sharing the base component and storing the adapter component in its inherent low-rank form, and further reduces compute overhead, enabled by shared-$A$ multi-LoRA architectures, by also sharing the low-rank cache and avoiding redundant computations for contexts already processed by other agents. To efficiently reconstruct adapter contributions at runtime, we introduce Flash-LoRA-Attention, a kernel that reorders attention computation to avoid materializing the low-rank cache to full dimension. LRAgent achieves throughput and time-to-first-token latency close to fully shared caching, while preserving accuracy near the non-shared caching baseline across agentic question-answering benchmarks.
tldr: The paper introduces LRAgent, a novel KV cache sharing framework for multi-LoRA LLM agents that reduces memory and compute overhead by decomposing the cache into shared base and adapter-dependent components, achieving comparable performance to full sharing with accuracy close to non-sharing.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了LRAgent，一种用于多LoRA LLM Agent的新型KV缓存共享框架，通过将缓存分解为共享的基础组件和依赖于适配器的组件，从而减少了内存和计算开销，实现了与完全共享相当的性能，并保持了接近非共享的准确性。
summary_zh: 多LLM智能体系统中的角色专业化通常通过多LoRA实现，即智能体共享一个预训练的骨干网络，并且仅通过轻量级的适配器进行区分。尽管共享基础模型权重，但每个智能体都为相同的、工具增强的长轨迹独立构建和存储其自身的 KV 缓存，从而产生大量的内存和计算开销。现有的 KV 缓存共享方法在很大程度上忽略了这种多LoRA设置。我们观察到，在不同的智能体之间，缓存差异主要由适配器的输出决定，而来自共享预训练骨干网络的激活仍然高度相似。基于这个观察，我们提出了 LRAgent，这是一个用于多LoRA智能体的 KV 缓存共享框架，它将缓存分解为来自预训练权重的共享基础组件和来自 LoRA 权重的适配器相关组件。LRAgent 通过共享基础组件并以其固有的低秩形式存储适配器组件来减少内存开销，并且通过共享-$A$ 多LoRA架构，进一步通过共享低秩缓存并避免为其他智能体已经处理过的上下文进行冗余计算，来减少计算开销。为了在运行时高效地重建适配器的贡献，我们引入了 Flash-LoRA-Attention，一个重新排序注意力计算以避免将低秩缓存物化到完整维度的内核。在智能体问答基准测试中，LRAgent 实现了接近完全共享缓存的吞吐量和首个令牌延迟，同时保持了接近非共享缓存基线的准确率。

title: Failure-Aware Bimanual Teleoperation via Conservative Value Guided Assistance
summary: Teleoperation of high-precision manipulation is con-strained by tight success tolerances and complex contact dy-namics, which make impending failures difficult for human operators to anticipate under partial observability. This paper proposes a value-guided, failure-aware framework for bimanual teleoperation that provides compliant haptic assistance while pre-serving continuous human authority. The framework is trained entirely from heterogeneous offline teleoperation data containing both successful and failed executions. Task feasibility is mod-eled as a conservative success score learned via Conservative Value Learning, yielding a risk-sensitive estimate that remains reliable under distribution shift. During online operation, the learned success score regulates the level of assistance, while a learned actor provides a corrective motion direction. Both are integrated through a joint-space impedance interface on the master side, yielding continuous guidance that steers the operator away from failure-prone actions without overriding intent.
